steps.title: Identify the circle's center and radius
steps.content: The equation $(x+3)^2 + (y-18)^2 = 169$ is in standard form. Reading directly: center is $(-3, 18)$ and radius is $\sqrt{169} = 13$.

steps.title: Find the range of possible x-coordinates
steps.content:
On a circle, the $x$-coordinate reaches its extreme values at the leftmost and rightmost points—each at distance equal to the radius from the center.

Leftmost $x$: $-3 - 13 = -16$
Rightmost $x$: $-3 + 13 = 10$

Therefore, for any point $(a, b)$ on the circle: $-16 \leq a \leq 10$

steps.title: Check each choice against the range
steps.content:
• A) $-17 < -16$ — outside the range
  • B) $-16 \leq -11 \leq 10$ — inside the range ✓
  • C) $13 > 10$ — outside the range
  • D) $18 > 10$ — outside the range
Only $a = -11$ satisfies the constraint. That matches choice B — B is correct.
